preamble: The shift from a voiced velar stop to a voiced palatal stop is a standard palatalization process, frequently seen in the development of Romance and Slavic languages.

examples.note: Latin /g/ palatalized to [ɟ] before front vowels, a precursor to the shift to [dʒ].
Initial stages of the First Slavic Palatalization of /g/ before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
